title: FEE_CLAIM
description: Message sent by the Referee to the Keeper to explicitly request payment for a completed assessment. The Keeper validates the FEE_RELEASE timer state and responds with FEE_CLAIM_RESULT. This pull-based mechanism complements the push-based FEE_RELEASE flow: a Referee MAY send FEE_CLAIM when the timer has elapsed but FEE_RELEASE has not yet been received. The Keeper MUST reject and respond with FEE_CLAIM_RESULT(REJECTED) if: the timer has not elapsed; a valid ASSESSMENT_APPEAL is pending; cert_id is not recognized (i.e. not found in an ASSESSMENT_ISSUED anchor for the Referee's own Keeper, or in an INCIDENT_NOTICE(ASSESSMENT_COMPLETE) record for a party Keeper in a split-Keeper topology); payment has already been released for this incident; or the escrow has transitioned to EXPIRED after the assessment deadline elapsed (STD-028). On acceptance the Keeper releases the full escrowed balance it holds — the Referee does not specify an expected amount. See RFC-0001 Section 6.22 and RFC-0002 Section 1.2 for details.

referee_id.description: Terminal ID of the claiming Referee.

cert_id.description: cert_id of the target CONTRIBUTION_RESULT. The Keeper cross-references this against the ASSESSMENT_ISSUED anchor.

currency.description: Three-letter uppercase currency code (e.g., 'USD', 'JPY', 'EUR'). ISO 4217 is recommended but not enforced. Used by the Keeper to record the currency context of this claim; the released amount and currency are determined solely by the escrowed balance.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when this claim was sent.

signature.description: Ed25519 digital signature over the canonical JSON of all fields excluding this field.
